g.data-narration: Welcome to Hyperledger - the leading enterprise blockchain platform by Linux Foundation
Hyperledger is an open-source collaborative project by Linux Foundation (2015) that advances cross-industry blockchain technologies
g.text: An open-source collaborative project by Linux Foundation (2015) that aims to
advance cross-industry blockchain technologies. Not a single blockchain, but an
umbrella project with multiple frameworks for enterprise solutions.

g.data-narration: Hyperledger enables global collaboration across finance, banking, IoT, supply chain, and manufacturing industries
Hyperledger has four key features: Modular architecture, Interoperable platform, Highly scalable, and Enterprise security
The modular architecture includes Consensus, Membership, Smart Contracts, Chaincode, Ledger, and APIs for customization
g.text: Allows developers to build custom blockchain applications using a range of different components:

g.data-narration: Hyperledger is designed to communicate seamlessly with other blockchain platforms like Ethereum and Bitcoin
g.text: Designed to communicate seamlessly with other blockchain platforms:

g.data-narration: Hyperledger handles enterprise-scale workloads with support for thousands of participants and high transaction throughput
g.text: Hyperledger is designed to handle enterprise-scale workloads:

g.data-narration: Enterprise security features include encryption, access control, key management, permissioned networks, auditing, and privacy
g.text: Hyperledger provides robust security features for enterprise applications:

g.data-narration: Three flagship Hyperledger projects: Fabric for enterprise apps, Sawtooth for IoT and supply chain, Corda for financial services
g.text: Three flagship projects designed for different industry needs:

g.data-narration: Hyperledger Fabric is the most popular enterprise blockchain with modular design, permissioned network, smart contracts, channels, and ledger
g.text: The most popular enterprise blockchain platform with modular architecture:

g.data-narration: Hyperledger Sawtooth uses PoET consensus, dynamic consensus, parallel execution, and JSON RPC API for IoT and supply chain
g.text: Designed for IoT, supply chain, and distributed energy markets:

g.data-narration: Hyperledger Corda is purpose-built for financial services with smart legal contracts, notary services, and regulatory compliance
g.text: Purpose-built for financial services and regulated industries:

g.data-narration: Real-world applications include supply chain management, financial services, healthcare, manufacturing, and government sectors
g.text: Use Cases - Supply Chain and Finance
Transparent tracking, verification, provenance of goods
Secure payments, cross-border transactions, KYC/AML compliance

g.data-narration: Key exam points: Hyperledger is an umbrella project, know the three main projects and their use cases, remember key features
g.text: Exam Tips - Key Points to Remember
Hyperledger is NOT a blockchain - it is an umbrella project with multiple frameworks
Fabric equals Enterprise apps, Sawtooth equals IoT/Supply Chain, Corda equals Financial Services
Key features: Modular, Interoperable, Scalable, Secure
Permissioned versus Public: Hyperledger requires invitation (unlike Bitcoin/Ethereum)
Security: Encryption, Access Control, Key Management

g.data-narration: Summary: Hyperledger is an open-source project by Linux Foundation with key features of modularity, interoperability, scalability, and security
